file_write_skip_abort.name: File-write: skip / abort decision
file_write_skip_abort.description: Every file write in init.ts checks existence first. If a file already exists the function throws an ABORT Error. Optional files (skill, hook) use copyTemplateIfMissing which returns 'created'|'skipped' and never throws.
file_write_skip_abort.source_anchor: packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:140-149 (top-level guards), L273-279 (writeNewFile helper), L281-289 (copyTemplateIfMissing helper)
file_write_skip_abort.inject_force_here: packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:140-149 — three existsSync guards before any work begins. To add --force, wrap each guard: `if (existsSync(X) && !args.force) throw new Error(...)`

citty_command_with_boolean_flag.name: Citty command definition with boolean flag
citty_command_with_boolean_flag.description: Commands use defineCommand({ meta, args, run }). Boolean flags use type:'boolean', default:false. Args type annotation is a local type alias. The run function destructures { args }.
citty_command_with_boolean_flag.notes: Hyphenated flags like check-only map to args['check-only']. For --force, use force (no hyphen) → args.force. Local type alias must be updated: type InitArgs = { target?: string; debug?: boolean; force?: boolean }.

citty_subcommand_registration.name: Command registration in allCommands router
citty_subcommand_registration.description: All commands are lazy-loaded via allCommands in commands/index.ts. Each entry is a function returning a dynamic import. The key becomes the CLI subcommand name (e.g. 'sync-meta' → fab sync-meta).
citty_subcommand_registration.rename_cost: Renaming fab→fabric is a one-line change at packages/cli/src/index.ts:14 (meta.name:'fab'→'fabric'). No subCommand registration changes needed. Binary name in package.json bin field is separate.

i18n_key_format_and_usage.name: i18n key format, catalog registration, and call site usage
i18n_key_format_and_usage.description: Keys follow dot-notation namespace: 'cli.<command>.<context>.<qualifier>'. Variables use {varName} placeholder syntax. Both en and zh-CN catalogs must have identical keys. The t() function falls back en→key if missing.
i18n_key_format_and_usage.key_naming_convention: cli.<command>.<area>.<qualifier> — examples: 'cli.init.args.debug.description', 'cli.init.created-path', 'cli.shared.skipped'. For --force flag: 'cli.init.args.force.description'. For force-overwrite message: 'cli.init.force-overwrite-path'.
i18n_key_format_and_usage.translator_signature: t(key: string, vars?: Record<string, string>): string — from packages/shared/src/i18n/create-translator.ts:17
i18n_key_format_and_usage.catalog_location: packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts and zh-CN.ts — these are the ONLY places to add new keys. The CLI imports createTranslator and detectNodeLocale from @fenglimg/fabric-shared.

error_abort_pattern.name: Error / ABORT throw pattern
error_abort_pattern.description: Errors are plain 'throw new Error(message)'. No custom error class or helper. ABORT messages use inline template literals with the file path embedded. The citty framework catches unhandled errors and prints them. There is one i18n key for abort-existing (added but NOT yet used in the code — current code still uses raw template literals).
error_abort_pattern.source_anchor: packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:141-149 (raw throw), packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts:104 (i18n key defined but unused: 'cli.init.errors.abort-existing')
error_abort_pattern.observation: The i18n key 'cli.init.errors.abort-existing' was added to both catalogs but the implementation still uses raw template literals. The --force fix should also migrate these to use t() — or leave raw for consistency with current code. Recommend using t() to be consistent with i18n intent.

test_pattern.name: Vitest test pattern for command handlers
test_pattern.description: Tests are in packages/cli/__tests__/*.test.ts. Framework is vitest with node environment. Fixture isolation uses createWerewolfFixtureRoot() (copies example project to tmpdir) + cleanupFixtureRoot() in afterEach. Command handlers are imported directly and called with .run?.({args}) — no CLI process spawn needed. i18n tests use vi.resetModules() + dynamic import to test locale switching.
test_pattern.no_e2e_infra: There is no end-to-end CLI spawn test infra — tests call exported handler functions directly. One exception: init-claude-install.test.ts:52 uses spawnSync to test the hook CJS script itself.

backward_compat_pattern.name: Backward compatibility / deprecation signals
backward_compat_pattern.description: No formal deprecation mechanism exists. The codebase uses CLIENT_ALIASES maps for client name normalization (claude/claude-code/claudecode all map to same canonical). One @deprecated JSDoc comment exists in scanner/forensic.ts for a transitional migration helper. Legacy meta format is handled silently in computeAgentsMeta (reads old format, writes new).
backward_compat_pattern.fab_to_fabric_cost: The binary name 'fab' is set in packages/cli/package.json bin field and in src/index.ts meta.name. Adding 'fabric' as alias: add 'fabric': 'dist/index.js' to bin in package.json. The meta.name in index.ts only affects help text. Citty has no built-in alias support for the root command name.

key_files.rationale: Primary target for --force flag injection: contains all three existsSync ABORT guards (L140-149), writeNewFile helper (L273-279), copyTemplateIfMissing helper (L281-289), and the initCommand defineCommand with args schema (L59-115)
key_files.topic_relation: This file IS the file-write pattern — every pattern to inject --force is defined here. The injection points are L64-73 (args), L75 (InitArgs type), L140-149 (guards), and writeNewFile L276.

key_code.description: Citty args schema with target:string and debug:boolean — add force:boolean here
Three ABORT guards before any work — primary --force injection points
Throws ABORT if file exists; needs --force bypass to call writeFileSync without the guard
Returns 'created'|'skipped', never throws — already has skip behavior, no change needed for --force

key_files.rationale: Defines allCommands map that registers all subcommands with citty. The key string becomes the CLI verb. Renaming fab→fabric requires no change here — only src/index.ts meta.name and package.json bin field.
key_files.topic_relation: Command registration pattern — shows how lazy dynamic imports hook into citty router. Adding a new command or alias would follow this pattern.

key_code.description: Record of command-name → lazy import function. All commands registered here. Hyphenated keys like 'sync-meta' become 'fab sync-meta'.

key_files.rationale: Root citty command definition with meta.name:'fab'. Rename fab→fabric requires changing this meta.name field and the package.json bin entry.
key_files.topic_relation: fab→fabric rename cost: single-line change here + package.json bin field. No subcommand registration changes.

key_code.description: 'fab' — changing to 'fabric' changes help text and tab completion. Binary rename is separate in package.json.

key_files.rationale: English i18n catalog — all new user-facing strings for --force flag must be added here. Key 'cli.init.errors.abort-existing' already defined (L104) but not yet used in code.
key_files.topic_relation: i18n pattern source — key format, interpolation syntax ({varName}), namespace structure all defined by existing keys in this file. New --force keys must follow same dot-notation pattern.

key_code.description: Existing abort key with {path} interpolation — already in catalog, not yet called via t() in init.ts

key_files.rationale: zh-CN catalog must mirror every key added to en.ts. Both catalogs are typed as Messages which is Record<string,string> — missing keys fall back to en then to the key itself.
key_files.topic_relation: Any new --force flag strings added to en.ts require identical keys added here with Chinese translation.

key_code.description: zh-CN version of abort key — '中止：{path} 已存在。fab init 是非破坏性的。'

key_files.rationale: Shows t() function signature and fallback behavior: activeMessages[key] ?? fallbackMessages[key] ?? key. Variables replaced via replaceAll('{name}', value).
key_files.topic_relation: i18n pattern implementation — interpolation uses {varName} not %s or ${}. All variables passed as Record<string,string> second argument.

key_code.description: Returns (key, vars?) => string. Falls back en→key. Variables interpolated with replaceAll('{name}', value).

key_files.rationale: Canonical test file for init abort behavior — directly tests the guards that --force bypasses. New --force tests should follow this exact structure.
key_files.topic_relation: Test pattern for --force: add new describe block 'initFabric --force behavior' that pre-creates blocking files then expects no throw when force:true is passed.

key_code.description: Drains tempRoots array and calls cleanupFixtureRoot — required teardown pattern for all init tests
Setup: write file, then call initFabric, assert throw with ABORT message — pattern to invert for --force test

key_files.rationale: Test helper utilities: createWerewolfFixtureRoot (isolated tmpdir with example project), cleanupFixtureRoot, writeFixtureFile, readFixtureFile — all new init tests reuse these.
key_files.topic_relation: Test pattern infrastructure — all init-related tests use these helpers. No new helper needed for --force tests.

key_code.description: Copies examples/werewolf-minigame-stub to tmpdir, removes AGENTS.md and .fabric to create clean init target
Creates file at relative path within fixture root — used to pre-seed blocking files in non-destructive tests

key_files.rationale: Vitest config with @fenglimg/fabric-shared alias pointing to shared/src/index.ts for in-source testing without build step. Test glob: __tests__/**/*.test.ts.
key_files.topic_relation: Test infrastructure — any new test files must go in __tests__/ with .test.ts extension. The shared alias means direct TS source is used in tests.

key_code.description: Maps @fenglimg/fabric-shared to ../shared/src/index.ts — new test files get this automatically

key_files.rationale: Best example of a boolean flag (check-only) with hyphenated name and how args['check-only'] is accessed in run(). Secondary example of the defineCommand pattern.
key_files.topic_relation: Citty boolean flag pattern reference — shows hyphenated flag in args object key, type: boolean, default: false, and access via args['check-only'].

key_code.description: Boolean flag with hyphen: type:'boolean', default:false. Accessed as args['check-only'] in run()

key_files.rationale: Contains nested subCommands pattern (bootstrap install) and CLIENT_ALIASES map showing how input normalization / backward-compat is handled without formal deprecation.
key_files.topic_relation: Backward-compat pattern: CLIENT_ALIASES maps many input strings to one canonical. fab→fabric rename analogy: no alias system exists in citty, rename is atomic.

key_code.description: Maps 'claude-code', 'claudecode', 'claudecli' etc to canonical 'claude' — the alias-not-deprecation pattern used throughout

force_flag_citty_hookup.description: Add force:boolean to initCommand.args in init.ts, pass through to initFabric() via updated function signature.

i18n_registration.description: New keys must be added to BOTH locale files before use. Keys are resolved at import time via t() which is called eagerly for meta.description fields.

fab_to_fabric_rename.description: Two-file change: src/index.ts meta.name + packages/cli/package.json bin field. allCommands in commands/index.ts unchanged. No citty alias mechanism for root command.

clarification_needs.question: i18n key naming for --force strings: should force-aware overwrite use 'cli.init.force-overwrite-path' or reuse 'cli.init.created-path' with a different label?
clarification_needs.context: The existing 'cli.init.created-path' key outputs '{label} {path}'. The label comes from createdLabel()/skippedLabel() helpers. A force-overwrite case could reuse this key with a new 'overwritten' label string, or add a dedicated 'cli.init.force-overwrite-path' key.
clarification_needs.recommendation: Add 'cli.shared.overwritten' label key (mirrors cli.shared.created/updated pattern) and reuse 'cli.init.created-path' template — minimizes catalog additions.

clarification_needs.question: Should --force tests be added to init-nondestructive.test.ts or a new init-force.test.ts file?
clarification_needs.context: init-nondestructive.test.ts tests the ABORT behavior. A --force flag inverts exactly those scenarios. Keeping them in one file documents the relationship but makes the file larger.
clarification_needs.recommendation: New file __tests__/init-force.test.ts — mirrors naming convention of init-nondestructive.test.ts and keeps concerns separated.

clarification_needs.question: Should initFabric() accept a force option inline, or should the command handler delete blocking files before calling initFabric()?
clarification_needs.context: initFabric() is exported and tested directly. Changing its signature (add options param) is clean but breaks any external callers. Deleting files in the command handler before calling initFabric() keeps the function signature stable.
clarification_needs.recommendation: Add optional options param: initFabric(target: string, options?: { force?: boolean }) — backward-compatible since options defaults to undefined/false.

summary.file_write_pattern: init.ts uses three top-level existsSync ABORT guards (L140-149) plus writeNewFile helper (L273-279). Force injection point: wrap each guard with && !args.force, add rmSync cleanup before writes.
summary.flag_pattern: defineCommand args with type:'boolean', default:false. Local type alias updated. Hyphenated flags use args['flag-name'].
summary.i18n_pattern: Keys in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts + zh-CN.ts. Format: 'cli.<command>.<area>.<qualifier>'. Variables as {varName}. Both catalogs must be updated simultaneously.
summary.error_pattern: throw new Error(message). i18n key 'cli.init.errors.abort-existing' exists in both catalogs but raw template literals still used in code — migrate to t() when adding --force.
summary.test_pattern: Vitest, node environment. Tests in __tests__/*.test.ts. Use createWerewolfFixtureRoot + cleanupFixtureRoot in afterEach. Call command handlers directly (not CLI spawn). No new test infra needed.
summary.backward_compat_pattern: No formal deprecation system. CLIENT_ALIASES pattern normalizes multi-name inputs. fab→fabric rename is a two-file atomic change with no alias support.
